assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md Steps 2-4 explicitly identify 'Root Cause 1: Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform' (shared/convention-utils.md) and 'Root Cause 2: Task creation uses Feature issue type ID instead of Task' (plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a). The independence assessment (lines 79-85) confirms they are independent, in different modules, affecting different code paths.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md line 105: 'This triggers the Decomposition Guard (Step 6)'. The independence assessment (lines 81-82) cites different modules (shared/convention-utils.md vs plan-feature/SKILL.md) and different code paths. decomposition-guard.md exists and is titled 'Decomposition Guard -- ACME-502' with 'This bug appears to involve multiple independent issues'.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 5-7: (1) 'Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (in shared/convention-utils.md)' and (2) 'Task creation uses Feature issue type ID instead of Task (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)'. Both issues are presented with specific file paths.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 9-11: 'Options: 1. Proceed -- create a single Task covering all fixes 2. Split -- I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md ends on line 13 with 'Choose (1/2):' -- an explicit prompt waiting for user input. No task creation occurred. investigation.md line 105 confirms: 'the user must decide whether to proceed with a single Task or split into separate Bugs'.
